name: Transcode & Replace
description: Batch-transcode library media to HEVC/AV1 and atomically replace the original file, with hardware detection, verification gates, HDR preservation and a persistent job queue.
overview: Transcode library media and replace the originals, safely.

versions.changelog: Fixes three bugs found in a full code review. Critical: after a dry-run pass, turning dry-run off never processed anything because the queue's duplicate check blocked every re-enqueue; skip verdicts are now retryable (except the expensive not-smaller verdict), so config changes and going live work as expected. Also fixes truncated ffmpeg output in the async process runner (could spuriously fail the VMAF gate) and the disk-space check inspecting the root filesystem instead of the media mount on Linux.

Reworked settings page. Cards are reordered into a natural flow with the master dry-run switch on top and the dangerous replace policy at the bottom above Save. Controls that don't apply right now (specific encoder, VMAF threshold, backup retention, the hard-replace warning) are hidden until they're relevant. Quality guidance now adapts to the chosen codec instead of a one-size HEVC hint. The settings that had no UI are now exposed: include/exclude path globs and a library multi-select, plus a Skipped counter and clickable detected-encoder chips. Library scoping is now actually enforced (and fails closed). Also fixes a stuck loading spinner on save errors and an empty-number-field footgun. 77 tests.

Review hardening from a full re-review. The VMAF quality gate now fails closed when the score can't be computed (libvmaf missing, ffmpeg error, resolution mismatch) instead of replacing the original anyway. The worker skips a source that already probes as the target codec, which self-corrects a job re-queued after a crash mid-replace. Fixed a 10-bit pixel-format false positive (yuv410p is 8-bit, not 10-bit). Added logging for a corrupt job queue and for permission (chmod/chown) failures, which matters on TrueNAS/SMB. Sealed the API controller. Also adds docs/COMPARISON.md comparing this plugin to Tdarr, Unmanic, FileFlows and HandBrake.

Restores the config page's pageshow handler so settings reliably load and save again (the dangling data-controller was the root cause; pageshow is the event the official Jellyfin template uses). Includes the 0.0.4 features: VP9 (libvpx-vp9, vp9_qsv, vp9_vaapi), Opus audio, a WebM container option, the detected-encoder list, a live processing/dry-run status and the dashboard sidebar entry.

Adds VP9 (libvpx-vp9, vp9_qsv, vp9_vaapi), Opus audio (transcode or add a compatibility track) and a WebM container option. Fixes the configuration page so settings load and save again, shows the detected encoders and a live processing/dry-run status, and adds the plugin to the dashboard sidebar.

Crash-safe replace: the original is backed up by hardlink/copy and the new file is swapped in with one atomic rename, so the source path is never empty; an interrupted replace is recovered on startup.
Redesigned settings page (grouped cards, Settings/History tabs) and a History view that shows each processed file with its poster, codec change and size saving.

First release. Full pipeline: hardware probe, persistent queue, discovery task, ffprobe verification gates, optional VMAF gate, HDR/Dolby Vision handling, playback/idle/disk guards and atomic replace with backup and permission preservation.
